summary: Exact frozen HEAD 42a6b66a627c passes runtime-contract review. The execution-state follow-up removes synthetic probe-Story resolution and derives PR synchronization ownership from the already selected canonical route template, preserving schema 0.2 named-profile routing, schema 0.1 flat and directory compatibility, managed/source transactional authority, rollback and concurrent-writer protection, and post-merge reconciliation. It adds no database, auth, environment, network, CLI reverse-call, runtime connector, or independent-review implementation.
inspection_summary: Inspected every changed path and the complete execution-state synchronization call surface; traced writeExecutionStateWithLinkedCopies through start, reconcile, PR-create, and PR-merge callers; compared schema 0.2 selected-route behavior with legacy schema 0.1 flat/directory behavior; reviewed transaction snapshot, commit-last, rollback, CAS, and recovery tests; reran execution-state tests 51/51; and verified exact-HEAD targeted 124/124, full suite 2028/2028, typecheck, docs build, and conformance 85/65/9/11 equal to the fetched origin/main baseline.
inspection_inputs: codebase-memory trace_path: writeExecutionStateWithLinkedCopies inbound/outbound depth 4 with tests
judgment_delta: Initial concern: removing the ownership probe could copy an entire shared PR directory and overwrite foreign Story artifacts -> Final conclusion: prRouteDirectoryIsStoryScoped examines the unresolved canonical template directory, treats only a directory containing {story_id} or {feature_slug} as scoped, and otherwise retains the explicit five-file lifecycle allowlist; existing foreign-authority and rollback tests remain green.

Initial concern: schema 0.2 catalog selection could still resolve an undeclared synthetic Story -> Final conclusion: managedWorktreeArtifactLocations now resolves only the selected state.story_id in managed and source authorities; the new named-profile unit and E2E fixtures require catalog metadata and Story mirrors and would fail under the pre-fix ownership-probe implementation.

Initial concern: the route change could break schema 0.1 compatibility or managed/source reconciliation -> Final conclusion: legacy routed flat-file, default story-directory, commit-last rollback, linked-only authority, CAS conflict, merge follow-up, and recovery-command tests all pass, including an independent 51/51 execution-state run.

Initial concern: the follow-up could duplicate Production Runtime Connectors or Independent Review Orchestration or add a run-session-to-CLI dependency -> Final conclusion: the only source modification is inside execution-state artifact routing; no connector/reviewer owner is added, imports remain directed to artifact-routing and existing execution owners, and current conformance exactly matches baseline at 85 total, 65 undeclared, 9 budget, and 11 orphan violations, including an unchanged pre-existing run-session-to-CLI count.
